Barcelona technical secretary Ramón Planes has made the club’s first public comment on the news that Lionel Messi has decided to leave the club.

Messi contacted Barça by burofax on Tuesday evening to announce his intention to depart and on Wednesday, the club held a press conference to unveil new signing Francisco Trincão.

Barça president Josep Maria Bartomeu was not present at the press conference but Planes was and, predictably, the technical secretary was asked about Messi’s future.

“It was very important news, we have always said that we want to build the new Barça around Messi, the best player in history,” Planes told reporters.

“We do not contemplate any contractual exit, because we want Messi to stay. Internally we are working for long hours to find the best solution for the club and for Leo.

“We cannot make this a public dispute because neither Messi nor Barça deserve it. We are working internally to convince Messi.”

Planes also insisted that Messi has NOT told the club that he won’t be joining up with the squad for pre-season training next week.

Reports elsewhere on Wednesday suggested the 33-year-old Argentine superstar has already talked with former coach Pep Guardiola about joining Manchester City this summer but as things stand, he is still very much a Barcelona player.
